commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- The program doesn't use any network functionality
- Keep -ltracker for BFilePanel

repository.description: Native Haiku GUI launcher for Claude Code

commit.message:
Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

commit.message:
Split gym leader Pokemon into individual rank-style rows

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add resilient upload error handling and self-chaining batch send

Frontend (Campaigns.tsx):
- On batch upsert failure, retry records individually to isolate bad rows
- Failed records are skipped, not blocking — upload continues
- Progress bar shows failed count alongside progress
- Launch toast reports how many records failed to upload
- Same fallback for campaign_contacts assignment errors
- Throws only if zero contacts were successfully assigned

Backend (send-campaign):
- Self-chaining: processes 50 contacts per invocation, then fires a
  new request to itself with the next offset (fire-and-forget)
- First call validates JWT + admin role; chained calls use service role key
- Balance check per batch to catch mid-campaign shortfalls
- Reads media type markers from templates.content (raw) since
  campaign.template_message is now marker-free
- Stops chaining if campaign status changes from "running"
- On last batch (fewer than BATCH_SIZE contacts), finalizes campaign
  status based on total sent vs failed message counts

commit.message:
perf: cache L2 consensus CSV load — eliminate 222x re-read per run

_load_raw_consensus_latest() was called once per market row inside
compute_l2_features(), re-parsing the entire 178K-line l2_consensus.csv
each time (40M rows/run). Now loaded once and passed through.

Reduces ~17 min bottleneck to ~5 seconds.

commit.message:
Enable Redis notification backend for cross-replica fanout

Without this, notifications only reach SSE connections on the same pod
that sent them. With 2 replicas, this means notifications rarely arrive.

commit.message:
MeetMeHalfway v2: Next.js 14 rewrite with venue types, travel midpoint, and live collaboration (#2)

* Update readme.

* update use yarn.

* Got local server running in react.

* Migrate to Next.js 14 with multi-stop, filtering, and voting

Replace the entire AngularJS + Express + MongoDB stack with Next.js 14
(App Router, TypeScript). Core flow: enter 2+ addresses, find centroid,
show nearby restaurants on a Leaflet map with Google Places API.

New features:
- Multi-stop support (N participants via Participant model)
- Google Places Autocomplete on address inputs
- Client-side venue filtering (price, rating, open status)
- Venue voting with shareable links (Vote model, toggle)
- Search history page
- Auto-detected DB provider (SQLite local, PostgreSQL production)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add venue types, travel-aware midpoint, and real-time collaboration

- Beyond Restaurants: users can search for cafes, bars, parks, libraries, gyms
  via pill selector; venue type stored in DB and shown in results/history
- Travel-Aware Midpoint: optional mode that uses Google Distance Matrix API to
  iteratively equalize travel times; supports driving/transit/walking/bicycling
  with graceful fallback to geographic centroid
- Real-Time Collaboration: polling-based live vote updates and viewer presence
  indicators; polls every 5s, pauses when tab hidden, stale viewers cleaned
  after 30s

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Integrate openclaw doctor into setup and operational stack (#28)

* Integrate openclaw doctor into setup and operational stack

Add openclaw doctor --non-interactive as a diagnostic tool across three
layers: post-install validation (setup skill step 11), daily preventive
scan (health check agent), and drift detection (machine-setup compliance
check). Health check agent diagnoses but never repairs — fixable issues
escalate to the debugger agent with doctor output. Includes rate limiting
(4h cooldown for problem-triggered scans), 60s timeout to prevent hangs,
and tail -10 in compliance script for sufficient diagnostic visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix macOS-incompatible timeout and unheld compliance script

Replace GNU coreutils timeout with portable perl alarm pattern.
The compliance script and health-check instructions now use
perl -e 'alarm 60; exec @ARGV' which works on default macOS.
Document the gtimeout alternative for machines with coreutils.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix unreachable fallback in compliance script diagnostics

Capture doctor's exit code before piping to tail — tail always exits 0,
making a trailing '|| echo' branch dead code. Now stores doctor output in
a variable, tails it, then checks the actual exit code separately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove prescriptive commands from machine-setup and health-check

These documents describe desired state for AI agents to interpret.
Replace perl timeout one-liners and implementation details with
declarative descriptions: what correct looks like, how to verify,
how to fix. The agents can figure out timeout strategies themselves.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

runtime: implement Supplier::Preserving and fix shared_vars array push (#1004)

* runtime: implement Supplier::Preserving and fix shared_vars array push

- Add Supplier::Preserving class (inherits from Supplier) with proper
  class definition and method dispatch
- Implement Supply.on-close callback registration and invocation
- Fix push_to_shared_var to keep env in sync with shared_vars — previously
  env.remove() left arrays invisible to closure env merge, causing react
  callbacks to lose pushed values after any start/await
- Support supplier_id-based polling in react event loop for live supplies
- Fix anonymous state variable ($) scoping for thread-local behavior
- Fix closure variable capture for sigilless aliases and sigiled names

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

The previous change that made anon_state_key always return None broke
anonymous state variables ($++) used in xx repeat thunks, causing
repeat.t tests 49-51 to regress.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: retrigger after flaky lock-async-stress failure

* ci: retrigger after flaky lock-async-stress failures

* fix: revert clone_for_thread shared_vars to or_insert to avoid race

Using sv.insert() in clone_for_thread could overwrite shared_vars
entries that were already updated by running threads, causing the
lock-async-stress test to fail intermittently.

fix(conference): resilient invite when suite_profiles missing

Root cause: invite endpoint hard-failed with 500 "Failed to load your
profile" when the inviter's suite_id had no matching suite_profiles row.
This happens for new/incomplete accounts or accounts using defaultSuiteId.

Fix: fallback to JWT-derived name (user_metadata.full_name or email
prefix) when suite_profiles lookup returns nothing. Invite proceeds
with partial display data instead of blocking entirely.

- livekit.ts: downgrade profile miss from error→warn, use inviterProfile
  fallback object with display_id from suite_id slice
- index.ts: attach authenticatedUserName from JWT metadata for fallback

commit.message:
fix: focus existing terminal window instead of opening new one (#64)

Replace lsof-based detection with AppleScript window title matching.
When opening a terminal, first try to find and focus an existing window
for that worktree using a deterministic [am] title prefix. Set explicit
window titles (with nickname/branch and unique ID) on new windows for
reliable matching. Supports Terminal.app, iTerm2, Ghostty, and Warp.

commit.message:
feat(validation): add Pydantic-backed validation across core models and geoms

- Add pydantic>=2.0 runtime dependency
- Convert Theme to pydantic dataclass: legend_position as Literal (catches
  typos instantly), base_size must be positive, ink/paper/panel_background
  validated as #RRGGBB hex strings
- Convert ColorScheme to pydantic dataclass: name and colors both validated
  as non-empty, consolidating checks previously scattered in factory functions
- Add __post_init__ to PlotSpec: width_mm/height_mm must be positive,
  dodge_width in (0, 2], padding must be 4 floats each in [0, 1]
- Fix adjust_size() and save_plot(): units typed as Literal["mm","cm","in"]
  and raises ValueError (not KeyError) on invalid input; negative dimensions
  now raise ValueError early
- Add explicit ValueError guards to GeomPoint (size>0, alpha∈[0,1]),
  GeomBar (width>0, alpha∈[0,1]), GeomDash (linewidth>0),
  GeomLine (linewidth>0, alpha∈[0,1]), GeomArea (alpha∈[0,1])

All 137 tests pass. mypy --strict and ruff report no issues.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Phases 6.5/7/7.5 pattern promotion, retrieval, and feedback tracing

Phase 6.5: Controlled agent-pattern promotion from converging workflow
learnings. Detects 2+ independent learnings sharing keywords, dedup
checks existing patterns, writes via bounded vault path.

Phase 7: Pattern-backed planner retrieval. Searches 20-agent-patterns/
specifically, reads full notes, extracts structured guidance sections,
injects compact advisory context into planning.

Phase 7.5: Planner feedback tracing. Records whether retrieved patterns
influenced planning and post-execution usefulness signal. Conservative
taxonomy (useful/neutral/unused/unknown/not_applicable). Logs to
LOGS/pattern_feedback.jsonl, never Obsidian.

222 total tests pass, zero regressions.

commit.message:
feat: implement orchestration kernel with agent registry, execution planner, and pipeline tracing

Add the core orchestrator that drives the WaibSpace agent pipeline:
- AgentRegistry for managing agent registration and lookup by category/id
- ExecutionPlanner that builds phase-ordered execution plans based on event type
- Orchestrator that processes events through the pipeline with parallel agent execution
- PipelineTrace for structured logging of execution timing and status
- Add @waibspace/agents as a dependency

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v19.2: Left sidebar sw-tile hover matches bottom picker style; revert overlay switches to original (3.2, scale5, no bg/border)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Strait of Hormuz closure scenario modeler

Interactive model with adjustable variables:
- Blockage % (0-100% of 21M bbl/d flow)
- Duration (1-180 days)
- Alternative route utilization (pipelines: 6.5M bbl/d max)
- SPR release toggle (4M bbl/d coordinated)
- Demand destruction toggle (ramps after week 2)

Outputs:
- Day-by-day price trajectory with canvas chart
- Peak/avg price, cumulative supply deficit, inventory depletion
- Portfolio impact table (14 instruments: futures, ETFs, stocks, options)
- 5 preset scenarios (brief, partial, extended, crisis, escalation)

Uses supply/demand elasticity model with fear premium decay,
demand destruction curve, and inventory buffer calculations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Implement MVP reasoning agents (IntentAgent + ConfidenceScorerAgent)

Add two reasoning-layer agents to the agent framework:
- IntentAgent: LLM-based intent classifier that categorizes user input
  into email, calendar, discovery, task, or general intents with entity
  extraction and downstream agent suggestions
- ConfidenceScorerAgent: rule-based confidence adjuster that refines
  intent classification scores based on entity presence, category
  clarity, input length, and reasoning ambiguity
